Guide May 18, 2026 • RecordWatchdog Team

How to Find Property Records and Liens in Your County

Whether buying a home or researching an investment, learn how to access property deeds, tax records, and mechanic's liens.

Advertisement

Google AdSense Unit

The Role of the County Recorder

Real estate and property records are maintained exclusively at the county level, typically by the County Recorder, Register of Deeds, or County Assessor. These government offices are legally responsible for documenting all transactions, transfers, and encumbrances related to real estate within their jurisdiction.

Finding Ownership and Tax History

Most county assessor websites offer a free, public property search tool. By entering a property address or parcel number, you can instantly find the current legal owner's name, the assessed market value of the property, square footage, and the complete history of property tax payments.

Searching for Liens and Encumbrances

A lien is a legal claim against a property to secure the payment of a debt. This can include mortgage liens, unpaid property tax liens, or mechanic's liens from unpaid contractors. These are recorded in the public record and can be searched through the county recorder's online database, often under the "Grantor/Grantee" index. Discovering a hidden lien before purchasing a property is crucial to ensuring the title is clear.

Why This Research Matters

Before purchasing a property, entering into a large financial agreement with a property owner, or extending credit, checking for outstanding liens is a fundamental step to ensure you will not inadvertently inherit hidden debts or legal complications.

Disclaimer

The information provided in this article is for general informational and educational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Public record systems and laws vary by jurisdiction and are subject to change. Always verify information directly with the official government agency or consult with a qualified attorney. RecordWatchdog is not a consumer reporting agency.
